reviews.review_msg: Make everything so complicated and teaching with all the AI knowledge, if you want to learn something choose other prof instead of. The most worst prof for this degree ever during 4 years
BUS 207 is tough course but Uzair's lecture and humor made it easy for me to understand.
Kind prof. His lectures were recorded which made it easier for me to score.
I failed his class but got A from  ECON103&105 with Martin. He is not teaching ECON, he is playing and randomly teach whatever he want…
Very tough grader and made all the class content more difficult and can't be accepted!
This man is too young. He tested and given the questions all from ChatGpt. He even don't know the answer, is that how qualified to be a Beedie Prof???
Everything is messed up and not make sense at all. He made me afraid BBA during the first year..
This guy just a PHD Student, don't know what to teach. Choose another prof instead…
He is a knowledgeable and caring  professor! Love his class!

BUS708. Extensive amount of mistakes. Lectures slides aren't organized well, with just mixed information point after point. Often wasn't able to understand student's questions. Uses exentsive AI. Disorganized, often not remembering various components of his own content. He's a smart well-meaning guy, but needs to refine his teaching methods.

Funny prof, lectures were engaging but not required, everything was recorded including tutorials. TAs didnt really explain what they were doing when answering practice problems, but it was clear enough.
Nice Prof. I liked the fact that practice problems and tutorials made attempting mid terms easy.
He is a really good prof given the fact that it is a 3 hr class and he keeps us engaged.
He is very friendly and always encourages questions.
Lots of content but exams are a summary of practice questions and basic econ.

He was fine, considering that he's new. Exams weren't too stressful as he said what would mostly be on them beforehand. Sometimes his hw solutions were done by AI and had incorrect answers or methods that were not taught in class.

The teaching is all jumbled up and there is no clear structure. Beedie actually assigned a PhD student to teach BUS207. A lot of the content was created by AI. Sometimes he had no idea about the students' questions. It's truly terrifying...

As a new professor honestly not bad. Funny and engaging prof. But the slides had a lot of incorrect information and the practice sheets were written by AI a lot which made it kind of confusing. But if you go to lectures and pay attention you should do fine.

Yall, he ain't bad AT ALL. I admit, the TAs actually suck, they explained nothing and just said what they wrote down. But Uzair is decent espesh considering the fact that this is his first time teaching. He's better than some of the experienced profs that I have had. So yeah, not bad all, don't let these negative reviews scare u. He's good dw.

Uzair doesn't deserve the hate he gets. I understand this is his first time teaching a full course but he is honestly better than some of the experienced professors i've had. His course is based fully on exams 25% midtermx2 and 50% final. People saying the tests are hard either didn't pay attention during lecture or didn't do the practice questions
made me not like economics even more. 0 teaching involved and the TA's write answers down with 0 explaination.

He's a “nice guy”, but being nice doesn't make you a good professor. He has an accent which makes him a bit hard to understand, is horrible at explaining concepts, doesn't use a textbook, instead makes his own lecture slides and they are horrible, uses his laptop curser to annotate instead of an iPad/tablet. inexperienced TAs do practice questions.
He is a nice guy. He always encourages us to ask questions. Plus he is quick to respond to any question asked.
He is not a good choice for this course, choose another instructor to instead of his section!
